entries.self_critique: Solved the core attribution: identified the PhaaS cluster, its shared TLS/JARM marker, the consistent registration recipe, and the RU bulletproof origin hidden behind Cloudflare, plus a cross-case link via the cert hash. But I left 123 pivots undrained, so the typosquat/subdomain surface is almost certainly incomplete, and the Cloudflare 522 blocked direct kit fingerprinting — template-matching rests on cert/JARM alone. Next time I'd kill dead OpenCTI pivots, run a deliberate CDN-origin-discovery step, and spend budget on cluster expansion before per-IP enrichment.

Wrap crt.sh in a small retry+jittered-backoff helper and emit a distinct failed status (not done) on 'non-list response' so gaps_report shows the transient error instead of silently counting it as completed.

Adaptive drain skip: if round-1 produced 0 new nodes AND 0 new edges, skip round-2/3 automatically (BOUNCE_PIVOT_DRAIN_CONVERGENCE already exists — extend to a hard zero-yield abort, not just a low-yield abort).

suggestions: Fix phantom_autostub: when add_edge references an unknown node id, infer type from the value's shape (domain regex / ipv4 / sha1) before falling back to value-as-type; never store type==value.

Add a 'cluster_confirmed' tag rule: when add_edge creates same_cert+same_jarm between siblings, mark both with cluster_confirmed and have pivot_mapping suppress threatfox/urlhaus/wayback/dnstwist/whois_domain on cluster_confirmed nodes after the first sibling pulls them.
Wire SpamHaus DROP/EDROP + an operator-ASN-count heuristic into a new bulletproof_host_score source; auto-tag operator nodes with >=3 ASNs under one registrant as bulletproof_hub.

Raise BOUNCE_PIVOT_QUEUE_MAX to 500 OR implement a per-(op,canonical_cluster) dedup key so 11 cluster-sibling domains don't enqueue 11×subdomain queries — the latter is the better fix.

entries.self_critique: Reached the right conclusion (Pitline bulletproof + star.* kit cluster + 9-domain shared-cert family) and built a tight, well-tagged graph, but only drained 35/337 pivot tasks before the queue ceiling and turn budget collapsed the run. blockers: Queue exploded to 340 pending / 177 deferred vs only 63 done — drain budget exhausted before high-value pivots (leakix_host x14, mnemonic_pdns x14, pulsedive_indicator x17, urlhaus_host x14) were ever attempted.
BOUNCE_TOTAL_CTI_BUDGET (default 82) is a flat cap that hurts ransomware/APT seeds where graph fan-out is legitimately wide; converged early on Interlock attribution but starved cross-corroboration.

Noise pre-filter only caught 3 skips (all admin@blnwx.com role mailbox); cloud-fronted shared-hosting siblings still spawned full pivot sets (crtsh_subdomains, virustotal_subdomains, dnstwist) that ate budget on co-residents unrelated to seed.

No source_dead flags fired — all 17 keys were functional — yet leakix/mnemonic/pulsedive (no-quota free sources) were never reached because they queued LAST behind 19 OpenCTI lookups and 14 wayback calls.

JARM/cert pivots converged on 6 JARM nodes + 3 certs but I never pivoted JARM → netlas_search / zoomeye_search to surface SIBLING Interlock C2 hosts globally — those are the highest-value pivots for ransomware infra and they sat undrained.
missing_capabilities: No tag-aware queue prioritisation: dom_fingerprints/wayback on shared-CDN domains drained before threatfox/urlhaus on the seed IP — pivot_mapping needs per-tag priority boost (malicious/c2/ransomware > historical > shared_hosting).

No batch IP-reputation endpoint: alienvault_reputation, project_honeypot_check, tor_exit_check, abuseipdb_check each fire one HTTP call per IP — 4 IPs × 4 sources = 16 calls when a single batched lookup would cost ~4.

No malware-config extractor for Interlock RAT / Supper / NodeSnake samples — had 8 hash nodes but couldn't pull embedded C2 URLs / mutexes / RSA keys without the original binary; static_analysis only fires on UPLOADED samples, not on hashes resolved via MalwareBazaar.

No 'sibling JARM cluster' pivot: JARM matches surface a single host but there's no `netlas_jarm_cluster(jarm) → top-N hosts` convenience that would auto-spawn ip nodes for the cluster — agents have to chain it manually and rarely do.

No way to mark a node as 'investigation boundary' (e.g. CDN noise reservoir) so its auto-enqueued pivots stop cascading — `defuse` only stops NEW pivots, doesn't kill the 30+ already queued under that node.
suggestions: Add `priority` column to pivot_tasks driven by source node's tags (c2/malicious/ransomware = +20, historical = -5, shared_hosting/cdn = -10); next_pivot ORDERs by priority DESC so high-signal work drains first.
Lower fan-out cap for cloud-fronted / shared-hosting parent nodes from 8/4 to 3/1 in pivot_mapping (the JARM cluster on 64.94.84.85 spawned 30+ pivots per co-resident).
Promote leakix_host, mnemonic_pdns, threatfox_search, urlhaus_host to MANDATORY-for-malicious-IP tier in agent_runner phase_followup — they're cheap, no-quota, and consistently miss the drain budget.

Add `mcp__cti__malwarebazaar_config(hash)` wrapper exposing MalwareBazaar's `get_file_information` config_extractor field — would have auto-surfaced Interlock C2 URLs from the 8 hash nodes without re-downloading samples.

Auto-cascade `defuse(node)` to mark all that node's pending pivot_tasks as skipped(reason='parent_defused') so killing a noise reservoir actually frees budget instead of just hiding the node visually.
noteworthy: Strong Interlock attribution chain: seed IP → 6 sibling JARMs → 8 malware hashes tagged Interlock RAT / Supper Backdoor / NodeSnake — convergence across MalwareBazaar + OTX + ThreatFox is unusually clean for a ransomware affiliate.

Repeat-infrastructure pattern on seed: tagged both `historical_c2_2025_02` AND `ssh_brute_force_2026_03` — same Hetzner IP recycled for distinct campaign phases 13 months apart, suggests operator-controlled rather than rented-on-demand.

entries.self_critique: Reached correct attribution (Interlock ransomware) with high-confidence threat_actor node, but left 84% of the pivot queue undrained — including the no-quota sources (leakix, mnemonic, pulsedive) that would have corroborated independently. Next time: triage queue by node-tag priority before draining, promote JARM-cluster pivots earlier, and treat shared-CDN siblings as boundary nodes from the start rather than letting them spawn 30+ child pivots each.

blockers: OpenCTI dead for entire run (auth_required): 25 lookup_indicator pivots skipped — every hash/domain/IP/email lost its community-KG attribution layer; the dead-source cache caught it, but the analyst lost free actor/campaign/ATT&CK labels.

Per-hash pivot fan-out exploded: 18 sibling Bumblebee hashes landed → queue ballooned to 150 pending (vt_file ×18, otx_file ×18, circl_hash_lookup ×18, mb_hash ×18). Drain budget burned on duplicates of an already-confirmed family verdict.

JARM 06d06d07d06d06d06c42d42d... is the generic non-discriminating fingerprint shared by tens of thousands of SoftEther / generic-TLS hosts — netlas_jarm/zoomeye_jarm pivots ran but yielded zero high-confidence siblings.

Revoked code-signing cert_serial 3805DC7EA4CD84151C90FFAF was a strong actor marker but no MCP tool exists to enumerate other samples signed with the same Authenticode publisher/issuer/serial cluster — had to leave it as a dead-end node.
AbuseIPDB returned 'clean' for 109.205.195.211 despite 13+ malicious samples beaconing to it — single-source reputation gave a false-negative; needed cross-source corroboration before trusting it.
missing_capabilities: No 'cluster-aware fan-out cap': when N sibling hashes attribute to the same family + same C2, enqueue enrichment for only K representatives (highest-confidence / most-recent) instead of N × per-hash ops.
No Authenticode/code-signing pivot: cert_serial node has no tool to find other binaries signed by the same publisher CN or revoked-cert cluster (would have surfaced more siblings deterministically).
No reverse-favicon / page-body-hash pivot from title_hash → other IPs serving the same SoftEther cover page (would have widened the bulletproof-hosting cluster on AS215381).
No ransomware-payment-tracking source for the Akira sibling (def3fe8d…): no chainalysis/ransomwhere/wallet lookup integrated, so payout/victim telemetry is invisible.
No SoftEther-instance-ID search: tracking_id 9C37197CA7C2428388C2E6E59B829B30 is a strong operator-identity marker but Censys/Shodan/Zoomeye searches by SoftEther GUID aren't wired as a pivot.
suggestions: pivot_mapping: add cluster-collapsing rule — when ≥10 hashes share an `attributed_to` edge to one threat_actor in the same investigation, cap per-hash enrichment fan-out to top-3 representatives; mark the rest 'skipped: cluster_collapsed' so gaps_report stays honest.

Add `cert_publisher_pivot` MCP tool: given cert_serial / Authenticode subject CN, query VT 'signed by' search + crt.sh issuer search to find sibling signed binaries — would deterministically widen the revoked-cert cluster.

Promote OpenCTI auth_required to a startup-time check (agent_runner pre-flight): if the token is dead, emit one analyst-visible warning at investigation start instead of letting every pivot skip silently — current dead_source cache hides the systemic outage.
Add a `softether_id_search` pivot (Shodan/Censys query `softether.id:<id>`) so SoftEther instance IDs become first-class operator-identity pivots, not free-text metadata.

When a JARM is the SoftEther/generic constant `06d06d07d06d06d06c42d42d...`, auto-tag `non_discriminating_jarm` at enqueue and downgrade jarm pivots to low-priority — saves two API calls per affected host.
noteworthy: Triple-family chain: Bumblebee loader → AdaptixC2 framework → Akira ransomware co-staged on the same domain 2rxyt9urhq0bgj.org / IP 109.205.195.211 — operational pipeline rather than separate intrusions.

Bulletproof-hosting indicator: AS215381 (Rockhoster, DE/IN) hosted the C2 + the SoftEther cover page + a shared-pubkey cert cluster across 4 separate certs — abuse@rockhoster.com tagged as takedown_target.
Banner masquerade: C2 listener served a generic SoftEther VPN landing page (title_hash 417b153f…) as cover; same body hash + tracking_id are operator-identity markers reusable across investigations.
Revoked but still-deployed EV code-signing cert serial 3805DC7EA4CD84151C90FFAF used to sign 8+ MSI lures (NetSetMan, Advanced IP Scanner, MIB Browser) — typical SEO-poisoning lure family.

entries.self_critique: Solved the case: family attribution (Bumblebee/AdaptixC2/Akira), C2 infra (IP+domain+ASN+certs), 18 sibling samples, takedown contact + operator identity markers all surfaced. Slowed by per-hash fan-out (queue hit 150 pending) and OpenCTI being dead. Next time: collapse same-family hash clusters early, run an OpenCTI sanity-ping at startup, and prioritise the SoftEther tracking_id / code-signing pivots that don't yet exist as MCP tools.

blockers: OpenCTI source dead (auth_required: 'GraphQL AUTH_REQUIRED — token expired/invalid'): 49 pivots skipped, removing every community-KG attribution / ATT&CK / linked-report lookup for the entire investigation.

Pivot queue exploded to 252 pending vs 30 done — every one of the 35 hash nodes auto-enqueued ~8 pivots (otx_file=34 pending, virustotal_file=34, circl_hash_lookup=35, malwarebazaar_hash=30) and drain budget ran out before reaching most.
BOUNCE_TOTAL_CTI_BUDGET (default 82) was clearly hit early: dom_fingerprints/title_hash captured for moonzonet but no favicon_hash, no JA3/JA3S, no wayback timeline for any of 35 hashes.

No source-health pre-flight: OpenCTI token expiry was discovered lazily on the first pivot, not at investigation spawn. Analyst gets no warning before drain wastes a phase enqueuing 49 doomed lookups.

suggestions: Add MalwareBazaar/OTX/VT *_batch tools and have pivot_mapping coalesce N pending single-hash pivots into one batch call when N>=5; this collapses ~100 calls into ~10.

Promote OpenCTI auth check to investigation start (one ping in phase_main bootstrap); if dead, suppress *all* opencti_lookup_indicator enqueues entirely instead of skipping each one later — would have stopped 49 redundant skipped rows.
Drop circl_hash_lookup from default fan-out on hashes already tagged 'malicious' — NSRL is a clean-software corpus; only run it as a *de-noising* pivot on unknown/suspicious hashes.

Add 'attribution-saturation' rule in pivot_mapping: when threat_actor edge present with confidence >=0.85, downgrade same-family hash pivots from priority high→low so operator-pivot (whoxy_reverse, emailrep, dnstwist on registrant) drain first.
Wire favicon mmh3 + form_action into dom_fingerprints on every live URL node (currently only title_hash was captured for moonzonet.com).
noteworthy: Three distinct revoked code-signing personas (Sife Software / Donald Gay / Flyland) issued by GoGetSSL & SSL.com — MuddyWater appears to burn fresh CA identities per stage rather than reuse a single persona; ssl_com_issued + gogetssl_issued together is a strong signpost.
BugSleep loader has 6 distinct DLL hijack variants in-graph (cloud_dll x5, odbcext x4, dbconfig x2) — modular family, supports detection rule generalisation on DLL name + xor key pattern.

Operator persona 'Johnik Makedonskiy' + burner email modafabiches@outlook.com tied to mazafakaerindahouse.info via RDAP registrant — strong human-attribution surface that warrants a dedicated 'registrant operator' pivot chain (whoxy_reverse + emailrep + breach corpora).

entries.self_critique: Reached strong attribution (MuddyWater/MOIS, 0.7+) with 64 nodes / 122 edges, three C2s, registrant persona and three signing personas — case is functionally solved. But pivot drain converted only 30/252 queued tasks (89% abandoned) because hash fan-out wasn't throttled after attribution saturated. Next time: cap same-family hash pivots once threat_actor confidence is high, and pivot budget into the registrant/persona branch instead.

blockers: Seed is Cloudflare-fronted (104.21.x/172.67.x); origin-enrichment on the 6 CDN IPs was defused (~114 skips), so true origin 217.145.226.186 was recovered only indirectly via cert/registrant clustering, not DNS.

Operator abuse/noc role mailboxes (abuse@altawk.com etc.) correctly noise-filtered (12 skips), so no reverse-WHOIS path from them; attribution rests narrowly on the single RIPE handle ALTAWK-DE1 / maintainer awk-mnt.
missing_capabilities: No reverse-search for verification/tracking tokens: the recovered google-site-verification ID is a strong operator fingerprint but no source clusters it across other domains.
No CDN-origin discovery chain: no historical/passive-DNS A-record lookup and no favicon->origin reverse-search to de-anonymize Cloudflare-fronted hosts (origin found only via cert reuse).
No live HTTP fetch / render / screenshot of the exact phishing path (/home/votenam) — lure-content analysis depends entirely on a pre-existing urlscan result.
tracking_id nodes are created but never pivoted: no op reverse-searches a recovered GSV/analytics ID even though favicon/JS-hash equivalents have search tools.
suggestions: Budget-aware drain order: when CTI budget is low, front-load cheap verdict feeds (threatfox/urlhaus/phishtank) on the phishing domains before bulk low-yield pivots (whois_ip/wayback/reverse_dns).
Collapse defused-CDN skips in gaps_report: 114 of 126 skips are identical Cloudflare-IP defusions that bury the 12 real noise-filter gaps — group them into one summary line.

CDN-fronted pivot rule: when a domain resolves only to a CDN, prioritize favicon-hash + reverse-favicon (Shodan/ZoomEye/Netlas) and historical passive-DNS for origin discovery instead of cert clustering.

Make tracking_id nodes pivotable: route recovered GSV/analytics tokens through an existing search source (Netlas/ZoomEye/urlscan dorks) so they cluster across domains instead of being dead-end markers.
Lower bulk IP doc-pivot fan-out on CDN ranges: whois_ip/rdap_ip/reverse_dns on Cloudflare IPs yield nothing yet inflate the pending queue (209) and starve drain budget.
noteworthy: Operator 'altawk' runs a self-contained bulletproof AS200823 (shell ASN) with its own RIPE maintainer (awk-mnt) + handle (ALTAWK-DE1) and abuse contacts on its own domains — the abuse desk IS the operator (takedown-resistant).
French voting/dancing-lure cluster (konkurzfrance.click, francedancingvotingfr.click, voteingmap.click), Cloudflare-fronted, RU bulletproof origin (217.145.226.186 / 185.107.75.25 / 77.91.97.169).
Operator naming tradecraft: lookalike domains altawk.com/.net + alwk.cc (contraction) plus a reused google-site-verification token (0Z2a4Y8...) act as a single fingerprint across the cluster.
Cert/JARM reuse was the de-anonymizing thread: cluster-anchor cert 0697888e... and mail JARM 2ad2ad... linked the Cloudflare-fronted phishing domains back to the RU origin despite DNS hiding it.
entries.self_critique: Core case solved: attributed the cluster to PhaaS operator 'altawk' on RU bulletproof infra behind Cloudflare via cert/JARM/registrant convergence. But budget ran out with 209 pivots pending, skipping cheap abuse-feed confirmations (threatfox/urlhaus/phishtank) on the phishing domains themselves. Next time: front-load verdict feeds and attempt favicon->origin de-CDN before slow cert clustering.

blockers: opencti_lookup_indicator = AUTH_REQUIRED on all 3 calls (cert 548b4f01, origin 217.145.226.186, seed domain): no OPENCTI_API_KEY, so community-KG actor/family attribution stayed empty.
urlscan_search('page.title:"School votingFR"') returned ~81k chars, untruncatable; MCP-only agent has no filesystem to slice it, so kit-sibling enumeration by page title was blocked.

shodan_host(217.145.226.186) = membership_required and criminalip_ip(217.145.226.186) = daily_limit 403 — both second-opinion port/cert scans on the origin were blocked.
Passive DNS structurally blind: VT + Mnemonic pDNS on the CF-masked origin both returned 0, leaving Onyphe TLS-SAN datascan as the SOLE origin-unmask path (single point of failure).
missing_capabilities: No field-projection or result-size cap on urlscan_search (and no urlscan_search_domains helper) — large title/kit queries are unusable for a filesystem-less agent.
No phishing-kit template fingerprint pivot: clustering by page-title / HTML-structure / form-action hash isn't a first-class tool, so kit-sibling discovery fell back to a raw urlscan dump.
No redundant origin-unmask source: only Onyphe TLS datascan can de-front a CF-masked origin here — no Censys cert->host or historical pre-CF A-record path as a fallback.
No harness-level response truncation/pagination across CTI sources — any oversized source payload is unrecoverable for an MCP-only agent with no filesystem read.
suggestions: Auto-mark pivot_tasks done when their CTI tool is called directly: this run left 187 'pending' + 0 'done', so queue_status/gaps_report couldn't show what was actually tried.
Stop requeue_missing fan-out per-sibling once a cluster is proven same-cert+same-registrant — re-running whois/rdap/crtsh per apex generated most of the 187 phantom tasks.
Add a fields/limit projection to urlscan_search (or a urlscan_search_domains helper) so a page.title kit query returns just task.domain instead of 81k chars.
Add known CDN JARMs (e.g. Cloudflare 27d40d40...525c) to the defuse list so JARM pivots auto-skip — the agent had to manually reason past this noise each run.
Fix dangling edge: seed URL -> 0345c50c7c9087ef (known_ioc) targets a node absent from the graph; add_edge should auto-create or reject a dangling dst so verdict evidence isn't lost.
noteworthy: Operator reused one multi-SAN LE cert (548b4f01) on BOTH the Cloudflare edge and the bulletproof origin — that origin-side reuse defeated the CF front and clustered all 3 apexes.
Repeat PhaaS infra: 9 prior investigations on origin 217.145.226.186, 7 on cert 548b4f01 (pre-tagged altawk/phaas/voting-themed) — a prime certstream-watchlist candidate.
Detection lag tracks apex age (voteingmap 11/91, konkurzfrance 2/91, francedancingvotingfr 0/91) — the freshest sibling is caught only by the cert/origin pivot, not reputation feeds.
Registrant split across Dynadot + reseller Global Domain Group (IANA 3956); French voting lures ('School votingFR'), staggered registration 2026-04-10/11 = one coordinated batch.
Bulletproof tell: origin AS205775 (Neon Core/TrafficTransitSolution/Partner Hosting) shows conflicting geo (RU/ES/DE/BR) and recurs across the prior 9 investigations.
entries.self_critique: Case solved: confirmed phishing_kit_cluster and unmasked the CF origin 217.145.226.186 via Onyphe TLS-SAN, closing the cluster at 3 apexes on three independent markers (origin cert + IP + Dynadot/GDG registrar). Next time: drive pivots through mark_pivot_done so the engine isn't left with 187 phantom 'pending' tasks, and grab a live DOM/favicon fingerprint earlier (CF-522 blocked it this run).

blockers: crtsh_subdomains pivot errored twice (transient HTTP/JSON failure) — no automatic retry/backoff, so a transient outage on a single source killed all CT-log subdomain enumeration
dnstwist binary not installed on host — local typosquat permutation pivot failed immediately, robbing the case of its single most relevant lateral-discovery vector for a typosquat-style phishing seed
OpenCTI auth failed (OPENCTI_API_KEY invalid/expired) — community-KG attribution / report-linkage signal was completely unavailable across the whole investigation
Seed domain has no DNS resolution and no VT/Pulsedive record — every IP/cert/host pivot returned empty, leaving the agent with only the bare seed node to reason from
No live URL to fetch meant dom_fingerprints (favicon/title/tracking IDs/JS hashes) was unrunnable — the strongest cluster-the-kit signals for phishing infrastructure were unreachable
missing_capabilities: No bundled/static-binary fallback for dnstwist — pivot is silently broken on hosts where the binary is missing; should ship a pure-Python permutation generator or document the dep in deploy.sh

No 'historical resolution' pivot for currently-dead domains — Wayback only catches HTTP snapshots; need passive-DNS historical A-record sweeps (SecurityTrails / DomainTools / Farsight) when current DNS is NXDOMAIN
No reverse-WHOIS-by-registrant pivot from a phishing-kit naming pattern (e.g. all 'ezpass-*-pay.{cc,com}' siblings) — Whoxy reverse exists for emails but not for fuzzy name/keyword sweep
No automatic retry/backoff on transient crt.sh failures — a single 5xx kills the pivot permanently instead of re-queueing it with exponential backoff
No tool to enumerate sibling typosquats from a brand-keyword list (ezpass, usps, fastrak, sunpass…) — would have surfaced campaign-scale cluster without needing the dead seed to resolve
suggestions: Add a Python-native dnstwist replacement (or vendor dnstwist as a wheel dep) so the permutation pivot never silently fails on minimal hosts; surface 'binary missing' as a startup health-check, not a per-pivot failure

Implement exponential-backoff retry (e.g. 3 tries, 2s/8s/30s) for crt.sh, OpenCTI and any source whose failure mode is transient — mark_pivot_done(status='failed') should be reserved for hard 4xx/auth errors
Health-check OpenCTI auth at investigation start and emit a single quota_state-style warning instead of silently failing on every pivot — the agent currently re-discovers the broken key per node
Add a 'brand-typosquat sweep' pivot: given a seed matching a known brand-impersonation pattern, fan out to dnstwist+certspotter+urlscan over the brand keyword, not just the seed string
Auto-enqueue 'historical passive DNS' (mnemonic_pdns is good but limited) when current dns_resolve returns NXDOMAIN — currently the dead-domain case yields zero lateral movement
noteworthy: Seed ezpass-tollbill-pay.cc matches the well-documented 2024-2025 US toll-road smishing campaign (USPS/EZPass/SunPass/FastTrak lure pattern on cheap .cc/.top TLDs) — pattern recognition by TLD+keyword could have triggered campaign-cluster pivots even with zero live infra

.cc TLD + tolls-keyword + already-taken-down (NXDOMAIN, no VT/Pulsedive history) is the canonical 'burned phishing domain' fingerprint — worth a dedicated tag and a different pivot strategy than 'live infra'

Domain had a known_ioc edge but no further enrichable infrastructure — pure IOC-list hits with no graph fan-out should arguably short-circuit to REPORT rather than draining 18 pivots that all return empty
entries.self_critique: Case was effectively un-investigable post-takedown: seed is NXDOMAIN with no VT/Pulsedive/cert history, so 18/23 pivots returned empty and the graph has 1 node. I did the right mechanical sweep but should have detected the 'dead-burned-phish' shape earlier (NXDOMAIN + IOC-list hit + no historical resolution) and pivoted to campaign-cluster reasoning (brand-keyword typosquat sweep) instead of draining per-node pivots that were structurally doomed.

blockers: Shodan host lookup failed with tier_restricted (no membership) — the single most useful banner/port view for an IP was unavailable
Censys host lookup failed (likely no API key / quota) — second banner source also unavailable, so we had zero direct port/service data
CriminalIP returned 403 'limit exceeded' — third IP enrichment source down at the same time
OpenCTI returned 'auth failed - no API key configured' on both ip and email lookups, removing community KG attribution entirely
Whoxy reverse-WHOIS returned 'Zero Account Balance' and EmailRep unauthenticated API is disabled, blocking the email-pivot branch on abuse@qatar.net.qa
missing_capabilities: No fallback free-tier banner source when Shodan+Censys+CriminalIP are all down (e.g. Onyphe datascan on a single IP, BinaryEdge, Fofa, or a built-in lightweight TCP/TLS probe)
No CGNAT/mobile-carrier heuristic that down-ranks pivots on national-telco ASNs early — agent had to manually skip 8+ ASN fan-out pivots one by one
Abuse-mailbox classifier missing: abuse@qatar.net.qa is the documented Ooredoo carrier abuse contact, but the engine still queued Whoxy/EmailRep/OpenCTI on it as if it were a registrant email
No pivot for ip→passive-port-history via Onyphe synscan/datascan keyed by ip (only ASN-level datascan exists), forcing reliance on Shodan
No way to mark an investigation 'benign clean IP' early and short-circuit the pivot-drain rounds — three drain rounds ran on an IP with zero malicious findings
suggestions: Add a free-tier banner fallback: call onyphe_datascan with ip:<value> filter (not just ASN) and/or a lightweight in-process TLS/HTTP banner grab when Shodan+Censys both error

In pivot_mapping, tag national-mobile-carrier ASNs (Ooredoo AS8781/AS42298, Orange, Vodafone, etc.) as 'carrier_asn' and auto-skip onyphe_datascan/shodan_search/netlas_search/whois_ip with skip_reason='carrier_asn_noise'
Classify emails by local-part ('abuse@', 'noc@', 'security@', 'hostmaster@') and auto-skip whoxy_reverse/emailrep/opencti with skip_reason='role_mailbox' instead of burning quota on them
Surface tier/quota status in next_pivot() so the agent sees 'shodan: tier_restricted, censys: no_key' upfront and doesn't enqueue them — currently we only learn after a failed call
Add an early-exit gate in phase_pivot_drain: if AbuseIPDB score=0, VT malicious=0, OTX pulses=0, Pulsedive risk=none, and node is on a carrier ASN, skip further drain rounds
noteworthy: 37.211.126.117 is a clean Ooredoo Qatar mobile/CGNAT IP — AbuseIPDB 0, VT 0/94, OTX clean, no pDNS hits, no URLhaus, no ThreatFox. Likely subscriber egress, not infrastructure
IP appears on TWO ASNs simultaneously (AS8781 + AS42298), both Ooredoo — RDAP and ip-api disagreed; this dual-ASN pattern is normal for large telcos but tripped duplicate ASN-level pivots
tor_exit_check, project_honeypot, alienvault_reputation all clean — convergent 'benign' signal that the engine never used to short-circuit
Six paid/quota'd sources (Shodan, Censys, CriminalIP, OpenCTI, Whoxy, EmailRep) all failed in the same run — single-investigation tier_restricted rate is dangerously high for a production CTI tool
entries.self_critique: Case is solved: seed is a benign Qatari mobile-carrier CGNAT egress IP with no malicious indicators across 19 sources. But I burned ~17 pivot slots on dead-end sources (carrier ASN enumeration, role-mailbox reverse-WHOIS) that should have been pre-filtered. Next time: detect carrier_asn + role_mailbox at enqueue time, surface API-key/tier state in next_pivot(), and add an early-exit when convergent clean signals appear.

blockers: Pivot queue ended with 478 pending / 0 done — the drain loop ran out of turns long before the auto-enqueued work was exhausted, leaving 33 crtsh_subdomains, 35 threatfox_search, 30 wayback and 30 whois_domain checks untouched across sibling apexes.

Cloudflare IPs (104.21.9.25 / 172.67.158.162) defused 38 IP-side pivots correctly, but no fallback was attempted (e.g. shodan_search by JARM or by cert SHA256) to recover origin IPs the CDN was masking.

Two edges reference orphan node IDs 0102014ef1d35db4 and 596098121930ea9c as 'known_ioc' but the nodes themselves are absent from the graph — add_edge silently accepted dangling targets, costing me an audit step.

crtsh / certspotter rotation for the WE1 issuer returns extremely noisy SAN sets (often 100+ unrelated tenants per cert) — without server-side filtering by SAN regex I had to manually triage every pivot which burned turns.
VirusTotal subdomain enumeration on *.za.com / *.ru.com / *.sa.com apexes is gated by premium tier — free quota only returned the seed-adjacent leaf, hiding the true breadth of the sibling cluster.
missing_capabilities: No pivot from a JARM fingerprint or cert SHA256 to 'all hosts currently serving this fingerprint' via Censys/Shodan — would have replaced ~40 of the defused Cloudflare-IP pivots with one high-yield query.
No URL-fetch / live HTTP probe tool — urlscan_result requires a pre-existing scan UUID, so I could not actively trigger a new scan against ifelse.rlcozx.es to capture the live AiTM kit DOM.
No DOM-fingerprint extraction from urlscan/Wayback HTML — title_hash nodes had to be hand-extracted from urlscan summaries; dom_fingerprints currently only runs on live hosts.
No bulk certstream / cert-rotation watcher — Tycoon2FA rotates WE1 certs every ~2 weeks and there is no scheduled re-pivot when a new cert serial appears for a tracked apex.
No 'expand SAN list' helper on cert nodes — given a cert SHA256 I had to round-trip through crtsh_serial → manual SAN parse → add_node per sibling, instead of one tool returning the deduped SAN set.
suggestions: Make the pivot-drain loop respect a 'budget per op-type' so 30 wayback calls on unrelated apexes don't starve the 1 whoxy_reverse call that would have unmasked a registrant — prioritise convergence pivots over breadth pivots.

Add a 'discriminating_marker' override that downgrades Cloudflare IPs to documentation-only the moment the seed's NS is *.ns.cloudflare.com — currently each new CDN IP enqueues then skips ~19 pivots, polluting queue_status.
Reject add_edge calls whose src/dst node IDs are not present, or auto-create stub nodes with a 'phantom' tag so the analyst can spot reference errors instantly.
Add a cti tool `cert_sans(cert_sha256)` that returns the unique SAN set + per-SAN first/last-seen, so cluster expansion is one call instead of N round-trips.
Wire the report's `pivot_suggestions` field into mark_pivot_done so the drain loop preferentially executes analyst-recommended pivots before generic auto-enqueued ones.
noteworthy: Tycoon2FA cluster shares one operator-registrar (Sav.com, LLC) + one Cloudflare JARM across 8 sibling apexes (thihekou.za.com, sutheha.za.com, waithopio.ru.com, wiodedrea.ru.com, drivxy.sa.com, chatiofea.ru, ifincelmax.ru, droplenia.es) — JARM+registrar+kit-template is a stronger cluster key than any single IOC.

Three decoy title_hash templates ('Craflow', 'Kate M', 'Okos-Inspired') are reused verbatim across sibling apexes — title_hash is currently the highest-signal kit-attribution pivot, more reliable than favicon hash on this kit.

ifelse.rlcozx.es / otp.rlcozx.es / nosql.rlcozx.es naming pattern (developer-keyword subdomains) matches the Tycoon2FA AiTM stage-naming convention documented elsewhere — worth promoting to a heuristic tag rule.

abid1337@yandex.com surfaced on tbmfwjjxm.com which is a *separate* operator cluster but shares the parked-redemption + DGA-style apex pattern — possible reseller / kit-customer relationship worth a follow-up investigation.

ifincelmax.ru still resolves to r01.ru expired-domain parking (31.177.76.144 / .80.144) yet retains historical Let's Encrypt certs — useful as a 'post-takedown survivor' signal to prove the operator abandons rather than rotates registrar.
entries.self_critique: Cluster attribution succeeded: rlcozx.es is confidently tied to a Tycoon2FA operator running ≥8 sibling apexes via shared registrar+JARM+decoy-templates. But I left 478 pivots undrained, never triggered a fresh urlscan on the live AiTM pages, and didn't attempt JARM/cert-based origin-IP unmasking behind Cloudflare. Next time: drain convergence pivots first, request a live urlscan, and run shodan_search by cert SHA256 before declaring the IP layer 'defused'.

blockers: Pivot queue ballooned to 610 pending with 0 done/skipped/failed recorded — either mark_pivot_done() was never called as I drained, or fan-out per added node outpaced drain rate. Cannot tell which from queue_status alone, and there is no per-task 'started_at' to debug stuck items.

VirusTotal free-tier 4 req/min throttled every hash/domain/IP enrichment; 18 hash nodes × 4 VT ops each = ~72 VT calls competing for a 4 rpm budget — most of the wall-clock cost of the run was waiting on VT.

No tool to fetch an actual malware sample by hash (only metadata). For multi-family hashes like 06f42fa9... with 13 conflicting family labels, I could not unpack/sandbox to disambiguate which YARA hits were real vs aggregated.

Certspotter / crt.sh subdomain queries returned the same 30+ id####.cfd siblings repeatedly across cert / serial / subdomain pivots — no dedup at the source layer, every sibling re-triggered the full pivot fan-out on add_node.

OpenCTI lookups returned 0 hits for nearly every IOC despite obvious public reporting on SvcStealer / Amadey / Diamotrix — the community KG instance is either stale or undersubscribed, so attribution had to be reconstructed from VT comments + URLhaus tags.
missing_capabilities: No sandbox / detonation tool (Triage, Hatching, ANY.RUN, JoeSandbox) — for a binary seed with 'packed_or_encrypted' + 'upx' tags, the highest-value next step is detonation, and the workflow has no path to it.

No PE/unpack-aware static-analysis MCP tool exposed to the agent — sample_analysis.py runs only at upload time on user-supplied bytes, so hashes discovered mid-investigation (e.g. dropped_by_amadey children) cannot get the same entropy/strings/imports treatment.

No ViriBack / abuse.ch C2 tracker MCP — I tagged 62.60.226.159 'viriback_c2_tracker' from a single OTX pulse mention; a direct ViriBack feed would have confirmed the panel type and family attribution.

No automated DGA-family classifier — the id####.cfd pattern is obviously a generator (29 siblings all co-resolving to one IP), but I had to spot it by eye instead of having a tool that says 'pattern id\d{4,5}\.cfd, 29 members, low Shannon, likely DGA'.

No favicon-pivot from the FastPanel hash -437285948 was actually executed despite being queued — netlas_favicon/zoomeye_favicon were pending. Hits would have surfaced sibling C2 panels across other ASNs and is one of the highest-yield pivots for panel-style C2.
suggestions: Add a pivot-queue heartbeat / TTL: if a pending task sits >N minutes without a mark_pivot_done call, auto-mark failed with reason='drain_stalled' so gaps_report surfaces drain bugs instead of hiding them as 'pending'.

Cap fan-out per *cluster* not per node: 29 DGA siblings each enqueuing 18 pivots = 522 tasks for one logical entity. After detecting a sibling pattern, collapse to 1 representative + 1 'pattern observed' note.

Promote the favicon_hash + jarm pivots to mandatory in phase_followup when an IP carries a 'fastpanel' or 'panel_default' tag — these are the cheapest, highest-yield panel-clustering moves and they got skipped here.

Wire malwarebazaar_hash 'download_sample' (or a Triage/Hatching submit) as a follow-up to malwarebazaar_hash when tags include 'packed_or_encrypted' — currently the workflow terminates at metadata and never gets to behaviour.

Make opencti_lookup_indicator return a 'kg_empty' flag distinguishable from a real miss, so the agent stops re-querying a stale KG for every node and instead falls back to VT-comment / URLhaus-tag attribution earlier.
noteworthy: Tight infra triangle: 4 IPs (62.60.226.159, 158.94.208.102, 178.16.53.7, 196.251.107.23/61) all serve /diamo/data.php — same SvcStealer panel cloned across AS214351 (FEMO) and AS202412 (OmegaTech/Seychelles), suggesting one operator running parallel C2 on two bulletproof LIRs for resilience.

Cert serial 5fb52b87fd1d8920677036b01cdf1f13edd is reused across 4 distinct id####.cfd domains — Let's Encrypt serial reuse like this is unusual and is a strong operator-cluster discriminator (worth a dedicated pivot rule).

JA3S 9500e243...3a45 carries an 'rdp_cert_fingerprint' + 'desktop_bul6k1u' tag — operator left a Windows RDP self-signed cert exposed; rare and high-confidence operator artefact, deserves cross-investigation correlation.

id86195.com (the only .com in an otherwise .cfd cluster) registered via MAT BAO (VN registrar, IANA 1586) while siblings used WebNIC — registrar split suggests operator hedging against takedown on either side.

Hash 06f42fa9... carries 13 family labels (cobalt-strike + icedid + ryuk + redline + ...) — almost certainly a packed dropper triggering aggregated YARA, not literally 13 families; the workflow needs a way to flag 'family-label inflation' before downstream tools take the list at face value.
entries.self_critique: Solved the case at the cluster/attribution level: identified SvcStealer+Amadey+Diamotrix operator running /diamo/data.php panels across two bulletproof ASNs, mapped 30+ DGA siblings, surfaced cert-serial and JARM discriminators. But I let the pivot queue balloon to 610 pending with 0 done recorded — either a drain bug or I stopped marking — and never executed the favicon pivot that would have widened the FastPanel cluster. Next time: drain or defuse explicitly, and collapse DGA-sibling fan-out before it consumes the queue.

blockers: Pivot queue ended with 1328 pending / 0 done — I drove enrichment from manual tool calls instead of next_pivot()/mark_pivot_done(), so gaps_report shows 0 skipped/failed and gives zero visibility into what was actually tried.

VirusTotal free-tier 4 req/min throttled every pivot involving virustotal_* (resolutions, communicating_files, subdomains across ~28 IPs and 50 domains) — each lookup serialised behind cooldowns instead of fanning out across rotated keys.

Wayback / urlscan_result gave HTML snapshots but I had no in-workflow way to fetch the live HTML body of suspected drainer landing pages — I could see fingerprints existed but not the JS bundle / wallet-prompt strings.

OpenCTI lookups for the cryptodrainer-tagged IPs / certs returned thin results — community KG had little on this specific Rabby-imposter cluster, so attribution relied on circumstantial Russian-ASN co-tenancy.

dnstwist generated 50 permutations but there is no batched verdict pipeline — each candidate still needs an individual dns_resolve + whois + vt round-trip, which is why 50 dnstwist_permutations entries are sitting unpivoted.
missing_capabilities: No tool to fetch + parse a live URL body (HTTP GET with JS-rendered DOM or even plain HTML) — I can fingerprint a page via dom_fingerprints but cannot read the script tags, wallet selectors, or RPC endpoints that prove drainer family.

No reverse-favicon / reverse-title-hash pivot across the *full* graph after enrichment — zoomeye_favicon and netlas_favicon exist per-seed but I cannot say 'find every other host that shares THIS favicon mmh3 hash globally' as a single batched call.
No wallet-address pivot from extracted JS — even if I scraped the drainer JS, there is no MCP tool to take an ETH/BTC address and check chainabuse / ransomwhe.re / etherscan tags.
No bulk JARM/JA3S clustering — netlas_jarm and zoomeye_jarm are per-fingerprint; I had multiple JARMs in this case and no way to ask 'group these and show shared infra'.
No 'historic resolutions diff' helper — virustotal_resolutions_domain dumps the list but I had to eyeball which IPs were active vs sinkholed vs parked across 50 domains.
suggestions: Make the agent prompt *mandate* draining the pivot queue via next_pivot() for at least N rounds before manual tool calls — currently it is easy to bypass the queue, which destroys gaps_report visibility for the analyst.

Add an http_fetch MCP tool (plain GET + optional headless render) gated by defuse_lists so live drainer JS can be inspected without the agent shelling out — the tool can return body + extracted IOCs + wallet addresses.
Add a batched_favicon_cluster MCP tool that takes a favicon mmh3 hash and returns every node already in the graph plus Shodan/Netlas/ZoomEye hits in one call — saves 8+ round-trips per cluster.
Auto-degrade VirusTotal pivots to malwarebazaar/otx/threatfox when 429 cooldown is active instead of just queuing more VT calls — the key_pool already knows the cooldown state.

Add a crypto_wallet_lookup MCP tool wrapping ransomwhe.re / chainabuse / etherscan tag APIs so wallet-address seeds (and wallets extracted from drainer JS) get actual chain context instead of being graph-only nodes.
noteworthy: Cluster is a Rabby Wallet impersonation drainer: 'rabby_at_hosting_asn' tag on AS48108 (virtualdc.ru) and 'cryptodrainer_hosting' tag on AS47196 (Garant-Park-Internet) point to a known Russian-VPS-hosted drainer-as-a-service operation.

Three distinct Russian VPS ASNs (AS47196, AS48108, AS56364) plus NL-based AS62240 (BL Networks) and AS63023 (GTHost) form the hosting fan-out — classic 'multi-bulletproof + cheap NL fronting' drainer pattern.
Mix of historic_cert and active certs across the same infra suggests rotation of TLS material while keeping the same IP/ASN footprint — operator is reissuing certs, not migrating hosts.
AS14618 (aws_ec2_iad) appearing alongside Russian VPS suggests an AWS-fronted redirector or staging step — worth a focused pivot on the AWS-side IPs vs the rest of the cluster.

The graph has multiple JARM fingerprints but they were not clustered into 'TLS-stack families' — likely 2-3 distinct nginx/Caddy server configs across the cluster, an analyst would want this grouping surfaced.
entries.self_critique: Reached a defensible attribution (Rabby-imposter cryptodrainer on Russian bulletproof + NL fronting) and tagged the right ASN/cert pivots, but I left 1328 pivots un-drained and bypassed the queue entirely, so gaps_report is empty and reproducibility is poor. Next time: drain the queue first, then escalate to manual; and push for an http_fetch + wallet-lookup tool before starting any drainer/phishing case.

blockers: Pivot queue exploded to 2035 pending tasks vs 0 done — fan-out from crtsh_subdomains (76 pending) and per-domain x ~15 IP/domain sources overwhelmed the drain loop within BOUNCE_PIVOT_DRAIN_MAX_TURNS budget

Seed 195.177.95.163 fronted by Cloudflare on related hosts (104.21.82.36, 172.67.152.167 auto-defused) — origin IP discovery requires non-defused pivots (favicon/JARM mass-search) which are deep in the queue and never reached
get_graph(compact=true) returned 69k chars exceeding token limit forcing file-read fallback — slows every retrospective pass on medium+ graphs
No structured `summary` text on skipped pivots in gaps_report (all empty strings) — the analyst can't tell at a glance WHY each Cloudflare IP was defused without re-reading defuse_lists
Pivot enqueue is breadth-first without a budget governor: ~50 new pivots per new node means a single subdomain enumeration call can multiply the queue 10x in one turn
missing_capabilities: No origin-IP discovery pivot for Cloudflare-fronted assets (e.g. SecurityTrails historical A-records, Shodan ssl.cert.serial cross-search, Censys parent-cert pivot to find pre-CF hosts)
No bulk/batched pivot executor — every IP enrichment is a separate MCP roundtrip even when sources (ip_api_batch_lookup exists but isn't wired into the auto-pivot rules)
No priority re-ranking on the pivot queue once the graph reveals a discriminating marker (e.g. when one favicon_hash clusters 10 hosts, those become higher-value than yet another crtsh_subdomains)
No 'queue-aware' fan-out cap that throttles new pivots once pending > N — current per-node caps don't bound the global queue
No way for the agent to mark an entire branch (e.g. all pivots under a defused CDN ASN) as low-priority and deprioritize without skipping outright
suggestions: Add a global pending-pivot ceiling (e.g. BOUNCE_PIVOT_QUEUE_MAX=300) — when exceeded, new auto-enqueues go to a `deferred` status and require explicit promotion. Prevents the 2035-pending blow-up seen here
Populate gaps_report `summary` field with the defuse reason (`cloudflare_cdn`, `parking`, etc.) so the lessons-learned phase can quote concrete signals instead of empty strings
Wire ip_api_batch_lookup into the auto-pivot rules: when ≥5 pending ip_api_lookup tasks exist, coalesce into one batch call
Add a pivot_mapping priority boost: discriminating markers (favicon_hash, jarm, cert_serial with rare issuer) should jump to the front of next_pivot() ahead of generic enrichment
Compact get_graph further (drop tags/confidence by default, expose them via get_node) or add a get_graph(format='stats') mode for retrospective phases that only need shape, not content
noteworthy: Seed IP 195.177.95.163 — investigation hit Cloudflare-fronted infrastructure early; defuse logic correctly identified 104.21.82.36 and 172.67.152.167 as CF edge IPs and skipped 19 pivots per IP (good signal)
Defuse coverage worked as designed: 38 pivots skipped == 2 CDN IPs × 19 enrichment ops, exactly matching the defused-skip rule. The skip mechanism is healthy; the queue governor is the weak link

Despite drain rounds running, by_op shows ZERO completed pivots in queue_status — suggests pivot_drain phase may have executed work that didn't flush to mark_pivot_done, or the queue was repopulated faster than drained
entries.self_critique: The investigation produced a large graph but left a 2035-task pivot backlog with 0 marked done — strongly suggesting either drain budget exhaustion or accounting drift. I should have pruned aggressively (one favicon/JARM cluster pass) instead of breadth-expanding subdomain trees that fed the queue exponentially. Next time: enforce a hypothesis-first loop that closes branches before opening new ones.

blockers: VirusTotal 4 req/min free-tier throttle is the dominant bottleneck when fanning out 27 sibling hashes — every virustotal_file/communicating_files call serialised behind it.
OpenCTI lookups returned empty for most Bumblebee/AdaptixC2 hashes despite strong external attribution, forcing reliance on VT/MalwareBazaar tags and manual family assignment.

JARM fingerprint 06d06d07…f61ac543d994271f is a known AdaptixC2 marker but neither netlas_jarm nor zoomeye_jarm returned a confidently-attributed cluster — pivot value is theoretical without a populated TLS-fingerprint index.

MalwareBazaar signature search is keyed on family tag, but the agent had no reliable way to enumerate sibling MSIs by code-signing serial 38 05 DC 7E A4 CD 84 15 1C 90 FF AF — certspotter_serial returned no certificate-issuance graph for the revoked cert.
Wayback / urlscan returned no archived captures of 2rxyt9urhq0bgj.org, blocking visual verification of the SoftEther masquerade landing page beyond the title_hash.
missing_capabilities: No first-class pivot for Authenticode code-signing certificate → other-signed-binaries: a 'reverse-imphash' or 'reverse-signer' lookup over MalwareBazaar/VT would have surfaced the full Xiamen Duohanbeiwei/Xisu signing campaign in one call.
No native YARA-on-corpus tool: agent could not run 'find every PE in MalwareBazaar whose imphash == c286badf6aeb314119702bd4433d79df' — had to infer the cluster manually.
No SoftEther-server fingerprint source (specific HTTP banner + cert SAN combo) to validate 'masquerade' tag against the rest of AS215381 / Rockhoster.
No automated link from revoked-cert serial → CRL distribution point → other revoked certs from same CA batch (would have hardened the Xiamen actor attribution).
No tool returns AdaptixC2 controller-config decryption / beacon-config extraction from a sample hash — family detection stops at YARA-name level.
suggestions: Add a `malwarebazaar_imphash` and `malwarebazaar_signer_name` MCP tool — both endpoints exist on MB's API and would replace ~10 manual VT queries with one call per pivot.
Add a `virustotal_signer` / `virustotal_search` tool exposing VT Intelligence-style 'signer:"Xiamen ..."' or 'imphash:...' queries when a paid key is present; degrade to MB on free key.

The pivot queue is broken in practice: 198 tasks queued, 0 drained — either the followup phase isn't calling next_pivot, or mark_pivot_done isn't being invoked after manual tool calls. Add an agent-side reminder in the system prompt that hand-executed pivots MUST call mark_pivot_done(op, node_id) so queue_status / gaps_report stay meaningful.
Auto-enqueue a `certspotter_serial` + `crtsh_serial` pivot when a hash node gains a `revoked-cert` tag or a cert_serial edge — currently only cert nodes trigger this.
When a JARM is tagged `c2_jarm`, automatically schedule netlas+zoomeye+censys JARM searches and persist the union as a `jarm_cluster` report node so analysts get the IP fan-out without prompting.
noteworthy: Single C2 IP 109.205.195.211 (Rockhoster DE / AS215381) services 13+ Bumblebee samples spanning months — high-value sinkhole/block candidate but AbuseIPDB under-reports and AlienVault has it unlisted, a clear blind-spot in commodity feeds.

Two Xiamen-based code-signing companies (Duohanbeiwei + Xisu) sign multiple Bumblebee variants with a revoked cert reused after revocation — strong indicator of an ongoing CA-abuse supply chain, worth flagging to CA/B Forum.

Domain 2rxyt9urhq0bgj.org is a DGA-style label served from a SoftEther-VPN-titled landing page acting as cover for AdaptixC2 — title_hash 'SoftEther VPN Server' is a high-fidelity hunting pivot across the JARM cluster.
Bumblebee → Akira ransomware precursor chain confirmed on this seed (akira_precursor tag) — caller should treat any host beaconing to this C2 as a pre-ransomware staging event, not generic malware.
SHA1 imphash c286badf6aeb314119702bd4433d79df ties 96452e31...88770 to the Bumblebee loader DLL family — single hunting pivot worth pre-loading into corporate EDR YARA.
entries.self_critique: Solved the core case: confidently attributed seed to Bumblebee → AdaptixC2 → Akira-precursor with infrastructure (109.205.195.211, 2rxyt9urhq0bgj.org), actor (Xiamen signers) and TTP (SoftEther masquerade, revoked-cert reuse). Weaknesses: I worked pivots manually instead of draining the queue, leaving 198 phantom 'pending' tasks and a useless gaps_report. I also did not validate the JARM cluster size or surface a concrete IoC blocklist node — next time, end with an action_export-ready summary node.

blockers: OpenCTI lookups failed twice with auth_required (OPENCTI_API_KEY missing) — lost the single best source for canonical malware-family attribution and ATT&CK linking; had to reconstruct from VT/OTX tags + external_report nodes.

VirusTotal free-tier 4 req/min throttled enrichment of 19 sibling hashes — only 2/19 got VT/MB/OTX/CIRCL done (see queue_status: pending=17 on each of those ops); breadth-first hash triage was impractical so I picked siblings by intuition.

Pivot queue starvation: 428 pending vs 8 done at end-of-run. The autonomous pivot-drain rounds converged early (likely on the new-node threshold) while 40+ ops still had unrun work — meaning ~98% of auto-suggested pivots were never executed.

No fetchable web/HTML content from MCP — CheckPoint, Hunt.io, Sekoia, CERT-IL reports could only be referenced as external_report nodes by URL, not actually ingested. IOCs from those reports had to be typed in by hand from memory of training data, not verified.

sharecodepro.com Amadey MSI URL is offline (http://sharecodepro.com/uploads/e93d371c8a19cb07.msi) — no archive/wayback retrieval tool in workflow surfaced the payload bytes, so the dropper hash chain stopped at f7c4bc58 and could not be walked further.
missing_capabilities: No built-in YARA / hash-set scanner for known C2 cert fingerprints — Sliver default ed25519 cert 8bfed666… and JARM 05d10d20…fcf6501a had to be matched from memory rather than via a discriminating_marker() rule. These belong in defuse_lists / pivot_mapping as positive (known-bad) markers, not just defuse-negative markers.

No reverse-HASSH / SSH-fingerprint pivot — got ja3s 671c5858…ec for 94.26.90.166 but no tool to search Shodan/Censys/Onyphe for other hosts presenting the same SSH server fingerprint; this would have surfaced co-resident MuddyWater infra cheaply.

No GreyNoise / Spur / residential-proxy detector — needed to decide whether 46.30.188.99:8443 'BugSleep' callbacks were operator-controlled or proxy-fronted; defaulted to assuming operator-controlled with no evidence.

No automated MITRE ATT&CK validator wired into the workflow — mitre_attack_candidates returns candidates but the agent must hand-pick which ones to keep; no programmatic check that, e.g., T1059.003 is supported by an actual cmd.exe/cscript artifact in evidence.

No 'register_known_ioc' helper — Sliver cert SHA256, MuddyRot URI patterns (/api/checkin, /api/cmd/), DinDoor Deno-runtime signature, and the BugSleep C2 port:8443 idiom are reusable fingerprints; they should land in a shared known-bad table the next investigation reads at startup.
suggestions: Make pivot-drain rounds drain by op-budget, not just by new-node convergence — e.g. keep draining while any op has >0 pending AND quota allows, with per-op caps to avoid VT runaway. Today's convergence test exits with 428 pending, which is a silent gap.

Add a 'same_family sibling enrichment policy' to pivot_mapping: when 15+ hashes are linked by same_family to a single seed, dedupe pivots so we enrich the K most-recent / most-detected siblings, not all 19. Right now every sibling re-queues full vt+mb+otx+circl fan-out and we run out of quota.

Ship a 'fetch_report_iocs(url)' MCP tool (or pre-cache the public threat-intel report corpus) so external_report references can be expanded into typed nodes automatically. Right now agents transcribe IOCs from memory which is unverifiable.

Add a 'wayback_fetch_payload(url)' helper that pulls archive.org or urlscan-DOM snapshots of malware-distribution URLs and computes their hash — so dead dropper URLs like sharecodepro.com/uploads/*.msi still yield a hash to pivot on.

Promote known-bad TLS/cert fingerprints (Sliver default cert SHA256, MuddyRot JARM, Cobalt-Strike default cert) to pivot_mapping.KNOWN_BAD_MARKERS so add_node auto-tags them with high confidence instead of relying on agent recall.
noteworthy: Confirmed Russian↔Iranian MaaS overlap: serialmenot.com (MuddyWater DinDoor C2) shares the 94.26.90.166 host with sharecodepro.com which is an Amadey/CastleRat/ChainShell distribution point (Russian crimeware-as-a-service). Same IP, same operator window — strong infrastructure-sharing signal between MOIS-aligned MuddyWater and Russian-aligned MaaS.

BugSleep C2 endpoint is HTTP (not HTTPS) on port 8443 — http://46.30.188.99:8443/api/checkin & /api/cmd/. Port 8443 is conventionally HTTPS; the cleartext HTTP-on-8443 idiom is itself a fingerprint and is worth a Snort/Sigma rule.

Deliberate dual false-flag personae: 'Johnik Makedonskiy' (Russian-aligned handle) and 'Ogwe Chibuike' (Nigerian academic registrant chibuike.ogwe.232986@unn.edu.ng) both appear as MuddyWater registrants on the same campaign cluster — likely scripted persona engineering to muddy attribution.

AS207043 + DEDIK SERVICES LIMITED (UK shell) + 3NT Solutions LLP — three independently bulletproof signals converge on the same operator. Bulletproof-hoster scoring should treat this triad as a high-confidence cluster rather than three separate weak indicators.

Sliver default ed25519 cert 8bfed666… on 157.20.182.49 + Sliver default JARM 05d10d20…fcf6501a = unmodified out-of-the-box Sliver build. Operator OPSEC failure worth flagging — and a cheap detection win for defenders.
entries.self_critique: Got to a strong MuddyWater attribution with Russian-MaaS link in 85 nodes / 104 edges / 8 external reports, but I drained only 8 of 436 enqueued pivots (~2%) — relying on depth-first reasoning over breadth-first queue work. With OpenCTI down I leaned on tag recall instead of verified data. Next time: drain queue aggressively before composing the narrative, and dedupe same_family fan-out so VT quota isn't burned on near-duplicate siblings.

entries.self_critique: I attributed the seed hash to Nimbus Manticore / UNC1549 (IRGC) and built a 54-node graph covering 14 Azure C2 domains, a Linode VPS, and an OnlyOffice delivery chain. However, the pivot drain loop ran 0 of 437 queued pivots — no WHOIS ages, no URLscan screenshots, no MalwareBazaar cross-refs on sibling hashes, no Shodan banners. Attribution is solid (VT community tags + ThreatFox), but infrastructure depth is ~10% of achievable. I should have created an actor node for UNC1549 at first attribution signal and suppressed cloud-platform pivot noise from the start.
